title: Closer to the Material, Ryo Lu | Compile 26
summary: Ryo Lu discusses how AI changes the building process and the risk of losing direct connection to code as material. He explores the tension between AI-assisted output and craftsmanship, advocating for maintaining visibility into implementation details rather than treating software as a black box.
writeup: Ryo Lu examines how AI changes the building process and the risk of losing direct connection to code as a working material. The talk frames a tension between AI-assisted output and craftsmanship, and argues for keeping visibility into implementation details rather than treating software as a black box. The core takeaway: staying close to the material preserves understanding even as AI handles more of the production.
